recommendation.title: Schedule C / Single-Member LLC
recommendation.summary: Good news. Tina can move forward on this business type right now.
reasons: You picked single-member LLC, and that usually means a Schedule C style return unless a different tax election was made.

items.title: Review inventory and COGS treatment
items.summary: Tina should keep inventory-related costs in review until she knows whether they belong in inventory, cost of goods sold, or current deductions.
items.suggestedTreatment: Do not let inventory-like costs flow straight into ordinary expenses without inventory treatment review.
items.nextStep: Confirm inventory method, year-end inventory, and any small-business simplification path.

items.title: Review intercompany and related-party treatment
items.summary: Tina should review related-party and intercompany activity before using those balances or flows in ordinary return-facing totals.
items.suggestedTreatment: Keep entity-boundary-sensitive items out of ordinary deductions and income until they are characterized cleanly.
items.nextStep: Confirm whether the amounts are loans, due-to/due-from balances, capital flows, or unrelated business activity.
